nodes.context: Brazilian criminal organization engaged in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and turf wars, controlling parts of Rio de Janeiro and expanded to other states and neighboring countries.
Rival criminal organization based in São Paulo, involved in drug trafficking and conflicts with Comando Vermelho.
Splinter group from Comando Vermelho, rival gang in Rio de Janeiro.
Splinter group from Comando Vermelho, result of a power struggle in the mid-1980s.
Colombian guerrilla group involved in cocaine trade, had a working relationship with Comando Vermelho.
Brazilian criminal organization involved in drug trafficking, rival to Comando Vermelho in the Amazon region.
Colombian militia group involved in conflict with Comando Vermelho in the Amazon region.

Brazilian criminal organization engaged in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, truckjacking, and turf wars. Formed in 1979, controls parts of Rio de Janeiro and has expanded to other states and neighboring countries.
Brazilian criminal organization, rival of Comando Vermelho. Designated as a narcoterrorist organization by Argentina in 2025 and by the US in 2026.
Brazilian criminal organization, rival of Comando Vermelho. Founder Ernaldo Pinto Medeiros (UÉ) was killed by Fernandinho Beira-Mar.
Brazilian criminal organization, result of a power struggle between Comando Vermelho leaders in the mid-1980s. Rival of Comando Vermelho.
Colombian guerrilla group that entered the cocaine trade after Pablo Escobar's death. Had business dealings with Comando Vermelho via Fernandinho Beira-Mar.

edges.evidence_quote: It has fought in several small-scale conflicts with rival gangs such as Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC)
edges.context: Comando Vermelho has fought in conflicts with PCC.

edges.evidence_quote: It has fought in several small-scale conflicts with rival gangs such as ... Terceiro Comando (TC), the latter itself the result of a power struggle between Comando Vermelho leaders in the mid-1980s.
edges.context: Comando Vermelho has fought in conflicts with TC, which splintered from CV.

edges.evidence_quote: After Pablo Escobar's death, the CV established a working relationship with the FARC, a Colombian guerrilla group which moved into the cocaine trade.
edges.context: CV established a working relationship with FARC, exchanging arms for cocaine.

edges.evidence_quote: the CV, PCC, Familia do Norte, and Colombian militia groups including the Border Command and the Carolina Ramirez Front have violently fought
edges.context: CV has violently fought with Familia do Norte in the Amazon region.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2016, a 20-year truce between the Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC), a rival organization based in São Paulo, and the CV broke down, sparking violent clashes.
edges.context: A 20-year truce between CV and PCC broke down in 2016, leading to violent clashes.
